message:
fcp-store/object_store: close put-vs-put quota TOCTOU window

`MemoryObjectStore::put` previously released the `objects.write()`
guard before bumping `used_bytes` via `fetch_add`. Between
`drop(objects)` and `fetch_add`, a concurrent `put` could acquire
`objects.write()`, observe `used_bytes.load()` still at the pre-commit
value, pass its quota check, insert, and then both `fetch_add`s would
push `used_bytes` past `max_bytes`.

Concrete interleaving (max_bytes=200, three 100-byte puts):
  T_A inserts (used 0→100), drops objects, fetch_add pending
  T_B acquires objects, reads used=100, 100+100<=200 ✓, inserts
  T_C acquires objects (after T_B drop), reads used=100 (T_B's
       fetch_add not yet executed), 100+100<=200 ✓, inserts,
       fetch_add → used=200
  T_B fetch_add → used=300 (200-byte quota silently violated)

Move `fetch_add` inside the `objects.write()` critical section so the
commit happens atomically with the insert. `delete` already keeps its
`fetch_update` inside the critical section; this patch makes `put`
match.

Closes flywheel_connectors-sfunf.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
